heading: Agreements with flood insurance pool
label: 4052 Agreements with flood insurance pool
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original a reference to “this title” meaning title XIII of , , , known as the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 90–44882 Stat. 572section 4001 of this titleAug. 1, 1968

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to enter into such agreements with the pool formed or otherwise created under this part as he deems necessary to carry out the purposes of this chapter.
the terms and conditions under which risk capital will be available for the adjustment and payment of claims,
the terms and conditions under which the pool (and the companies and other insurers participating therein) shall participate in premiums received and profits or losses realized or sustained,

the maximum amount of profit, established by the Administrator and set forth in the schedules prescribed under , which may be realized by such pool (and the companies and other insurers participating therein),section 4018 of this title
the terms and conditions under which operating costs and allowances set forth in the schedules prescribed under may be paid, andsection 4018 of this title
the terms and conditions under which premium equalization payments under will be made and reinsurance claims under will be paid.section 4054 of this titlesection 4055 of this title

no insurance company or other insurer which meets the requirements prescribed under , and which has indicated an intention to participate in the flood insurance program on a risk-sharing basis, will be excluded from participating in the pool,section 4051(b) of this title
the insurance companies and other insurers participating in the pool will take whatever action may be necessary to provide continuity of flood insurance coverage or reinsurance by the pool, and

any insurance companies and other insurers, insurance agents and brokers, and insurance adjustment organizations will be permitted to cooperate with the pool as fiscal agents or otherwise, on other than a risk-sharing basis, to the maximum extent practicable.
